channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Shirin Hakim, Eric Schmitt and Qasim Nauman

item.title: Analysis of Satellite Image and Videos Suggest Precision U.S. Strikes on Iranian Water Facility
item.description: It is unclear if the U.S. intentionally struck the facility or knew what it was. Deliberately targeting civilian infrastructure could constitute a war crime.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl and Christiaan Triebert
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity

item.title: Trump’s ‘Secret Mission’ to Ferry Oil Past Iran Was Widely Disclosed
item.description: A U.S. military official said the president’s seemingly dramatic announcement on Wednesday referred to a previously reported effort to shepherd commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Jonathan Swan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, seen from Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran for Second Day, as War Heats Back Up
item.description: President Trump said that the Iranians were taking “too long to negotiate” a deal to end the conflict launched by the United States and Israel four months ago.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Max Bearak, Jonathan Swan and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government rally in Tehran, the Iranian capital, this week.

item.title: Gulf Arab nations condemn new Iranian attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: People walk past a mural depicting drones attacking a Star of David in the Iranian capital, Tehran, on Monday.

item.title: Drinking Water Facilities Hit by Strikes in Iran, State Media Reports
item.description: Two water tanks serving thousands of people were damaged, the local authorities said. The U.S. military declined to comment.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands, Sanam Mahoozi and James McManagan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Indian Sailors Made Frantic Calls for Help After U.S. Missile Strike off Oman
item.description: The 24 crew members were stranded for more than two hours before being rescued by an Omani military helicopter. The U.S. military said that the ship had violated its blockade of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Oman. An Omani military helicopter was used to rescue 24 Indian sailors aboard a ship that was struck by a U.S. missile on Monday.

item.title: Oil Rises on Trump’s Iran Threats and New Strikes Across Middle East
item.description: The attacks, which strained a two-month cease-fire between Iran and the United States, were the latest jolt to energy markets.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Quiet Oman Landed Itself in Trump’s Cross Hairs
item.description: As the Iran war drags on, Oman — a U.S. ally and mediator with Iran — has found itself at odds with the Trump administration and some of its own neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Fishing in Muscat, Oman, in February, before the war between the United States, Israel and Iran started.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Shirin Hakim and Lara Jakes

item.title: What to Know About the Sea Drone That Rescued Downed Apache Crew
item.description: It was the first U.S. rescue carried out by an autonomous surface vessel and remotely piloted by a human operator, according to a military spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Trump Vows to Retaliate for Downed Helicopter
item.description: The retaliatory U.S. attacks came on Tuesday as Israeli forces pounded southern Lebanon while targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman, Euan Ward and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “The United States must, of necessity, respond to this attack,” President Trump said on social media before the strikes.

item.title: 6 Countries Announce Sanctions Targeting Israeli Settler Networks
item.description: The measures aim to “disrupt the flows of finance” supporting attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank, said the British government, which coordinated them with France and other nations.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: An attack by settlers in October on two Palestinian brothers in Huwara, in the West Bank.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Trump Vowed to Retaliate for Downed Army Helicopter
item.description: President Trump vowed to retaliate for what he said was Iran’s downing of an Apache helicopter. near the Strait of Hormuz. The pilot and gunner were rescued.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels anchored in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman.

item.title: Iran’s Soccer Federation Says Its World Cup Tickets Have Been Revoked
item.description: It did not say who had interfered but blamed the United States, one of the tournament’s hosts, after months of war. FIFA said it was looking for a solution.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran’s players arriving on Sunday in Tijuana, Mexico, where they will be based for the World Cup.

item.title: Israel Strikes Lebanese City of Tyre as U.S.-Brokered Truce Falters
item.description: The bombardment followed Israeli evacuation warning for the entire city, a day after Iran threatened to attack Israel again if it kept up its offensive against Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Netanyahu’s Bow to Trump’s Iran Pressure Spurs New Criticism
item.description: Opponents attacked Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for halting strikes against Iran after a call with President Trump, saying that he was letting the United States make Israel’s decisions.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at the Knesset, Israel’s parliament, last week.

item.title: A Challenge in the U.S.-Iran Talks: Both Sides Demand Victory
item.description: Washington and Tehran would need to defend any potential deal as a win for their side. And each has a leader whose approach to talks is vexing mediators.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Farnaz Fassihi and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in Tehran on Monday after Iran and Israel exchanged fire.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Farnaz Fassihi and Euan Ward

item.title: Stocks Drop on Tech Fears and War Worries
item.description: Oil prices fell after Israel and Iran agreed to halt strikes against each other, easing fears of a broader regional conflict that had briefly driven crude higher.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Roar of ‘Cease-Fire’ in a Once-Thriving City
item.description: The city of Nabatieh once spoke to the changing fortunes of Lebanon. Now, amid unending war, it speaks to the country’s worst fears.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Hwaida Saad and Daniel Berehulak
item.media:description: An empty road to Nabatieh, in southern Lebanon, last week.

item.title: Crew Is Rescued After U.S. Helicopter Goes Down Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It was not immediately clear what led to the incident, which came at a time when the cease-fire in the war has grown tenuous.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A U.S. Army Apache helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump Wants to Call the Shots. But in Iran, He Keeps Hitting His Limits.
item.description: President Trump is grappling with his own version of the sort of Middle East crisis that beset his predecessors, and that he promised to avoid.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump insists that he has not violated his campaign pledge to avoid “endless wars,” even though a conflict he called “a little excursion” in March has entered its fourth month.

item.title: Iran and Israel Pull Back, After Fierce Exchange of Attacks
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, an Iranian-backed militia, is complicating efforts to end the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Ronen Bergman and Maggie Haberman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard of former ayatollahs Khomeini and Khamenei in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Iran-Israel Escalation Ignited in Lebanon, Which Is Trapped in Their Conflict
item.description: The power struggle between Iran and Israel to define the new rules of the Middle East plays out in Lebanon, which the other two countries have used to pursue their own ends.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers on Sunday at a damaged building in a neighborhood on the southern outskirts of Beirut, a Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: Are U.S. and Israel on the Same Page in Mideast Wars?
item.description: President Trump has voiced his frustration with Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, but it is not clear how able he is to rein in Israeli military action.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has found himself on the sidelines of talks to end the war in Iran.

item.title: The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
item.description: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Jes Aznar for The New York Times
item.media:description: The president of Solaric, a rooftop-solar installer based near Manila, said people were now seeing the technology as “a way to survive this new age.”

item.title: Houthis Threaten to Widen Iran War by Blocking Israeli Shipping in Red Sea
item.description: The Yemeni militia group, which is backed by Iran, said it would impose a partial blockade in the waterway, a move that would further strangle supply routes and aggravate the conflict in the Mideast.
item.dc:creator: Shuaib Almosawa and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Houthi supporters in Sanaa, Yemen, sit around an Iranian flag in April at a demonstration in solidarity with Tehran.

item.title: After new attacks, Israelis lament a ‘never-ending loop of war.’
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sheltering underground during an air raid siren in Tel Aviv on Monday.

item.title: Why Iran Risked an Attack on Israel
item.description: Iran’s leaders want to show they are serious about defending their Hezbollah allies in Lebanon and maintaining the regional balance of power, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of a projectile that landed in northern Israel, early Monday morning.

item.title: What to Know About Mahshahr, the Iranian Petrochemical Complex That Israel Targeted
item.description: The facility makes materials that Iran says are for civilian uses, but that Israel argues are also deployed in military applications.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Part of the Mahshahr petrochemical complex in southern Iran in 2011.

item.title: The 15-Hour Fight With Iran Showed the Bind Israel Is In
item.description: As it braces for a possible U.S.-Iranian peace deal, Israel now knows that if it responds forcefully to attacks by Hezbollah, Iran may strike Israel with missiles.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel in Jerusalem in March.

item.title: Europe Watches Its Economic Recovery Fade Into the Distance
item.description: As the war in Iran persists, signs point to a prolonged period of higher prices and slower growth rather than a quick shock.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development
item.media:credit: Violette Franchi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A supermarket in Paris. For Europe, the economic drag is now forecast to last into next year.

item.title: No Calm for Tehran’s Residents as Iran-Israel Fighting Resumes
item.description: As explosions sounded in the Iranian capital, people frantically checked phones and social media to see whether they were under attack again.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman, Ronen Bergman, Jonathan Swan and Max Bearak

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Katie Rogers

item.title: Tribeca Festival Denounces Pair Who Joked on the Red Carpet About Rape
item.description: A clip of the actor Elon Gold with Lizzy Savetsky, a social media influencer, at the premiere of “The Wedding Entertainer” has been circulating online.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Johnny Diaz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times; Rob Kim, via Getty Images for Tribeca Festival

item.title: Iran Fires Missiles at Israel for First Time Since April Cease-Fire
item.description: Israel had struck the outskirts of the Lebanese capital, Beirut, earlier on Sunday, prompting Iran to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Iranians Sink Into Despair Facing War Deaths and Skyrocketing Inflation
item.description: An imploding economy is causing hopelessness among both pro- and anti-government Iranians. And for those who wished for regime change, the letdown is palpable.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran in April. Daily life in Iran, already difficult, has grown harder in the months since the war began, with rampant inflation, civilian deaths and damaged infrastructure.

item.title: Israel Bombs Beirut Outskirts as Fighting With Hezbollah Escalates
item.description: U.S. efforts for a truce in Lebanon appear to have stalled. Israel accused Hezbollah of firing at Israeli territory. Iran swiftly retaliated.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Rescue workers are seen on Sunday at a damaged building in a neighborhood on the southern outskirts of Beirut, a longtime Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: OPEC Plus to Boost Oil Production as Ceasefire in Iran Remains Elusive
item.description: The cartel’s move to increase output by 188,000 barrels per day is largely symbolic, with vast amounts of the world’s oil stranded by the effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Friedman
item.category: Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman al-Saud, Saudi Arabia’s minister of energy, left, and OPEC’s secretary general, Haitham Al Ghais at an economic forum in St. Petersburg, Russia, last week.

item.title: Arab Gunman Kills One in Attack on Central Israel
item.description: The assailant, an Arab citizen of Israel, was killed. Hamas, the Palestinian militant group, praised the shooting, but it did not claim responsibility.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:description: Israeli security forces in Kochav Yair, Israel, on Sunday, after a shooting.

item.title: Trump Says Iran Has Made a ‘Big’ Nuclear Promise. It Isn’t New.
item.description: President Trump’s boasts of securing a commitment from Iranian leaders not to develop a nuclear weapon have puzzled nuclear experts who note that Tehran has made that pledge for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump has said the main reason he went to war on Feb. 28 was to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon.

item.title: Trump’s Defense Department Sees Growing Espionage Threat From Israel
item.description: The Defense Department has increased the counterintelligence threat assessment to its highest level, and Israel is believed to have eavesdropped on American negotiations with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu last December. The United States and Israel were largely aligned at the war’s outset, with Mr. Trump endorsing Mr. Netanyahu’s goal to oust the Iranian government.

item.title: Infant Killed as Israeli Military Fires on Car in West Bank, Palestinian Officials Say
item.description: Israel’s military said a soldier opened fire after Israeli troops “perceived a vehicle accelerating toward them.” The baby’s grandmother, who was in the car, disputed that account.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Fahd Abu Haykal during the funeral of his 7-month-old son, Sam, in Hebron in the Israeli-occupied West Bank on Saturday.
